description: Copilot SQL Assessment - 100 scenarios covering selection, projection, filtering, sorting, limits, joins, aggregation, calculations, subqueries, and response shape. Base tables with JOINs only. No views.

Scenarios.Question: Show me all tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show me some tickets with ticket number and title only.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: List the latest tickets in the system.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show unresolved tickets only.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: Show resolved tickets only.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ResolvedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets that are still active.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE s.IsClosedState = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets that have SLA breached.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.IsSlaBreached FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsSlaBreached = 1

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets that do not have SLA breached.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.IsSlaBreached FROM Tickets t WHERE ISNULL(t.IsSlaBreached, 0) = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with an external reference.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ExternalReference FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ExternalReference IS NOT NULL AND t.ExternalReference <> ''

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets without an external reference.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ExternalReference IS NULL OR t.ExternalReference = ''

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, and created date only.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, status name, and priority name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id LEFT JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, entity name, and assigned agent name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName, u.FullName AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, category name, and source name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, c.Name AS CategoryName, src.Name AS SourceName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId = c.Id LEFT JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId = src.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, due date, and resolved date.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate, t.ResolvedAt FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, escalation level, and approval status.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.EscalationLevel, t.ApprovalStatus FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket title and description for open tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.Title, t.Description, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE s.Name = 'Open'

Scenarios.Question: Show only user full name, username, and email.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 u.FullName, u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u
CorrectAnswer.Note: Must not expose password or security fields.

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number with parent ticket number.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.TicketNumber AS ParentTicketNumber FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Tickets p ON t.ParentTicketId = p.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number with configuration item name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, ci.Name AS ConfigItemName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN ConfigurationItems ci ON t.ConfigurationItemId = ci.Id

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with Open status.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE s.Name = 'Open'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with In Progress status.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE s.Name = 'In Progress'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with Critical priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id WHERE p.Name = 'Critical'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with High priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id WHERE p.Name = 'High'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets assigned to Support Team.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, tm.Name AS TeamName FROM Tickets t JOIN Teams tm ON t.TeamId = tm.Id WHERE tm.Name = 'Support Team'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets created by admin user.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, u.FullName AS CreatedBy FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id WHERE u.UserName = 'admin'

Scenarios.Question: Find unassigned tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AssignedToUserId IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: Find unassigned open tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE t.AssignedToUserId IS NULL AND s.Name = 'Open'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets from Finance entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id WHERE e.Name = 'Finance'

Scenarios.Question: Find Critical tickets from Finance entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName, e.Name AS EntityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id WHERE p.Name = 'Critical' AND e.Name = 'Finance'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets where title contains error.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE '%error%'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets where title or description contains server down.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.Description FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE '%server down%' OR t.Description LIKE '%server down%'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets mentioning database or DB.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE '%database%' OR t.Title LIKE '%DB%' OR t.Description LIKE '%database%' OR t.Description LIKE '%DB%'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets mentioning network, connectivity, or connection.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.Description FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE '%network%' OR t.Title LIKE '%connectivity%' OR t.Title LIKE '%connection%' OR t.Description LIKE '%network%' OR t.Description LIKE '%connectivity%' OR t.Description LIKE '%connection%'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets containing urgent or critical keywords.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id WHERE t.Title LIKE '%urgent%' OR t.Title LIKE '%critical%' OR t.Description LIKE '%urgent%' OR t.Description LIKE '%critical%'

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets created today.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE CAST(t.CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(GETUTCDATE() AS DATE)

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets created yesterday.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE CAST(t.CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(DATEADD(day, -1, GETUTCDATE()) AS DATE)

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets created this week.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(day, -7, GETUTCDATE())

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets created this month.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(month, -1, GETUTCDATE())

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets created between 2026-01-01 and 2026-03-31.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-03-31'

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets resolved in the last 24 hours.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ResolvedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETUTCDATE())

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets escalated in the last 48 hours.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.EscalatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.EscalatedAt >= DATEADD(HOUR, -48, GETUTCDATE())

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets due within the next 7 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate IS NOT NULL AND t.DueDate BETWEEN GETUTCDATE() AND DATEADD(day, 7, GETUTCDATE()) AND t.ResolvedAt IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: Show overdue unresolved tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate IS NOT NULL AND t.DueDate < GETUTCDATE() AND t.ResolvedAt IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets older than 30 days and still open.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE t.CreatedAt < DATEADD(day, -30, GETUTCDATE()) AND s.IsClosedState = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show top 10 most recently created tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show oldest 20 unresolved tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 20 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets sorted by priority then created date.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id ORDER BY p.SortOrder ASC, t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets sorted alphabetically by title.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.Title ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets by due date ascending.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.DueDate ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show only the first 5 tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 5 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show latest 25 resolved tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 25 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ResolvedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show top 15 tickets with longest resolution time.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 15 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(HOUR, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS HoursToResolve FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY HoursToResolve DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show top 10 entities with most tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 e.Name AS EntityName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show top 20 agents by assigned ticket count.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 20 u.FullName AS AgentName, COUNT(*) AS AssignedCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id GROUP BY u.FullName ORDER BY AssignedCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with status names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with priority names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with entity names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with assigned agent full names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, u.FullName AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with creator full names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, u.FullName AS CreatedByName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with creator and assignee names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, creator.FullName AS CreatedByName, assignee.FullName AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers creator ON t.CreatedByUserId = creator.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers assignee ON t.AssignedToUserId = assignee.Id

Scenarios.Question: List comments with ticket number and author name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, c.Content, u.FullName AS AuthorName FROM TicketComments c JOIN Tickets t ON c.TicketId = t.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON c.CreatedByUserId = u.Id

Scenarios.Question: List attachments with ticket number and file name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, a.FileName, a.FileSize FROM TicketAttachments a JOIN Tickets t ON a.TicketId = t.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with category, status, priority, and entity names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, c.Name AS CategoryName, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName, e.Name AS EntityName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId = c.Id LEFT JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id LEFT JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with impact and urgency names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, i.Name AS ImpactName, u.Name AS UrgencyName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketImpacts i ON t.ImpactId = i.Id LEFT JOIN TicketUrgencies u ON t.UrgencyId = u.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with workflow step names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, w.Name AS WorkflowStepName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketWorkflowSteps w ON t.CurrentStepId = w.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with SLA level names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, sl.Name AS SlaLevelName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketSlaLevels sl ON t.SlaLevelId = sl.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with source names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, src.Name AS SourceName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId = src.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with department and location names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, d.Name AS DepartmentName, l.Name AS LocationName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Departments d ON t.DepartmentId = d.Id LEFT JOIN Locations l ON t.LocationId = l.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with approver names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, a.FullName AS ApproverName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers a ON t.ApproverUserId = a.Id

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are in the system?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: How many users are in the system?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS UserCount FROM AspNetUsers

Scenarios.Question: How many comments exist across all tickets?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS CommentCount FROM TicketComments

Scenarios.Question: How many attachments exist across all tickets?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS AttachmentCount FROM TicketAttachments

Scenarios.Question: How many open tickets exist?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS OpenCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE s.IsClosedState = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many resolved tickets exist?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ResolvedCount FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets breached SLA?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS BreachedCount FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsSlaBreached = 1

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by status name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT s.Name AS StatusName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id GROUP BY s.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by priority name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT p.Name AS PriorityName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by entity name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by category name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT c.Name AS CategoryName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId = c.Id GROUP BY c.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by source name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT src.Name AS SourceName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId = src.Id GROUP BY src.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by assigned agent.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT u.FullName AS AgentName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id GROUP BY u.FullName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by creator name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT u.FullName AS CreatorName, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id GROUP BY u.FullName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by month created.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') ORDER BY Month

Scenarios.Question: Count tickets by year created.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT YEAR(CreatedAt) AS Year, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets GROUP BY YEAR(CreatedAt) ORDER BY Year

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with days open.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, ISNULL(t.ResolvedAt, GETUTCDATE())) AS DaysOpen FROM Tickets t ORDER BY DaysOpen DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show resolved tickets with hours to resolution.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(HOUR, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS HoursToResolution FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY HoursToResolution DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show average resolution time in hours.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(HOUR, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt)) AS AvgResolutionHours FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: Show average resolution time by priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT p.Name AS PriorityName, AVG(DATEDIFF(HOUR, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt)) AS AvgHours FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.Name

Scenarios.Question: Show average resolution time by entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, AVG(DATEDIFF(HOUR, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt)) AS AvgHours FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL GROUP BY e.Name

Scenarios.Question: Show SLA breach rate by priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT p.Name AS PriorityName, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN t.IsSlaBreached = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) * 100 / COUNT(*) AS BreachRate FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id GROUP BY p.Name

Scenarios.Question: Show SLA success rate by hour of day.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT DATEPART(hour, t.CreatedAt) AS HourOfDay, COUNT(*) AS TotalTickets, SUM(CASE WHEN t.IsSlaBreached = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS BreachedTickets, 100.0 - (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN t.IsSlaBreached = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) * 100 / COUNT(*)) AS SlaSuccessRate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(month, -1, GETUTCDATE()) GROUP BY DATEPART(hour, t.CreatedAt) ORDER BY HourOfDay

Scenarios.Question: Show total estimated hours by status.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT s.Name AS StatusName, SUM(t.EstimatedHours) AS TotalEstimatedHours FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id GROUP BY s.Name

Scenarios.Question: Show total time spent per ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, SUM(te.HoursSpent) AS TotalHours FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketTimeEntries te ON t.Id = te.TicketId GROUP BY t.TicketNumber, t.Title

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with more than 5 comments.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId = t.Id) > 5

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with no comments.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId = t.Id)

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets with no attachments.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketAttachments a WHERE a.TicketId = t.Id)

Scenarios.Question: Show a ticket summary with friendly names, not raw IDs.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName, e.Name AS EntityName, assignee.FullName AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id LEFT JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId = p.Id LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers assignee ON t.AssignedToUserId = assignee.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC
CorrectAnswer.Note: Must return friendly names instead of raw foreign key IDs.

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket analytics grouped by entity with total tickets, open tickets, closed tickets, and SLA breach rate.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, COUNT(*) AS TotalTickets, SUM(CASE WHEN s.IsClosedState = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS OpenTickets, SUM(CASE WHEN s.IsClosedState = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ClosedTickets, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN t.IsSlaBreached = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) * 100 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS SlaBreachRate FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId = e.Id LEFT JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId = s.Id GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY TotalTickets DESC
